rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to ports or interface behavior; use implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Do not encode AADL property names like Period, Compute_Execution_time, Priority as AGREE variables unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be syntactically valid and placed in appropriate AADL component scopes.
Avoid generating clauses that reference AADL properties as AGREE variables without explicit model support.
Ensure all AGREE expressions use types and identifiers that are present in the AADL model scope.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes unless they are explicitly defined in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use exact Boolean expressions for Boolean ports; avoid numeric encodings of event ports.
anti_patterns: Do not assume AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
Do not invent numeric bounds (e.g., x >= 0) unless explicitly required by the requirement.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
Model Analyst Agent's role in extracting AADL structure
